steps.title: Connect comments to expectations
steps.content: The passage says comments are closely related to customers' expectations about service. Gas stations near schools usually offer discounts, but gas stations near restaurants do not. The lower good-to-bad comment ratio near restaurants should be explained by expectations shaped by the lack of discounts.

steps.title: Reject unsupported explanations
steps.content: Choice A discusses customer volume, not comment ratios. Choice C only restates that school-area stations receive relatively better comments. Choice D proposes adding more stations near schools, which does not explain the observed pattern.

steps.title: Choose the expectation link
steps.content: The remaining choice explains that restaurant-area customers may have higher expectations because they pay higher prices without discounts. The answer is B.
